\noindent We now regress $y$ = \ttblue{revenue} on $x_1$ = \ttblue{production}, $x_2$ = \ttblue{coolDD}, and $x_3$ = \ttblue{heatDD}.

\par\addvspace{\topsep}

\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_blue}]
ussteamco_mod_1 = fit_lm('ussteamco_mod_1', 'revenue ~ production + coolDD + heatDD', USSteamCoEstim)
summary_model('ussteamco_mod_1')
\end{Verbatim}

\par\addvspace{\topsep}
\noindent Adding the additional variables \ttblue{coolDD} and \ttblue{heatDD} has improved the fit of the model. The residual standard deviation decreased from 4,112,487 for \ttblue{mod.0} to 1,980,698 for \ttblue{mod.1}. Similarly, $r^2$ improved from 0.397 to 0.868. These metrics suggest that the inclusion of relevant variables enhances the explanatory power of the model, demonstrating the importance of carefully chosen criteria in stepwise selection methods.
